name: Algol (Beta Persei)
description: Algol is the prototype eclipsing binary system. Its primary eclipses cause a dramatic brightness drop of about 1.3 magnitudes every 2.87 days, making it ideal for educational photometry exercises.

name: Beta Lyrae
description: Beta Lyrae is a semi-detached eclipsing binary with continuous brightness variation due to ellipsoidal distortion of the stars. Its light curve shows smooth, sinusoidal changes.

name: W Ursae Majoris
description: W UMa is a contact binary where both stars fill their Roche lobes. Its very short period of about 8 hours makes it possible to observe a full cycle in a single night.

name: Delta Cephei
description: Delta Cephei is the prototype Cepheid variable. Its brightness varies with a characteristic rapid rise and slow decline, governed by the period-luminosity relation used to measure cosmic distances.

name: RR Lyrae
description: RR Lyrae is the prototype RR Lyrae variable, a pulsating star found in globular clusters. Its short period and asymmetric light curve are distinctive.

name: Mira (Omicron Ceti)
description: Mira is the prototype long-period variable. Its enormous brightness range of over 8 magnitudes makes it visible to the naked eye at maximum and invisible at minimum.
